question_text: A student heats ice from −10°C until it becomes steam at 110°C. During which part of this process is the temperature NOT increasing, even though heat energy is being added?

options.label: A
options.text: When the ice is warming from −10°C to 0°C

options.label: B
options.text: When the water is warming from 0°C to 100°C

options.label: C
options.text: When ice is melting at 0°C and when water is boiling at 100°C

explanation: During changes of state (melting and boiling), all the heat energy goes into breaking bonds and changing the state, not into raising temperature. This is called latent heat.
